name: Spotlight Search
description: Opens the Spotlight search bar for searching files, apps, contacts, calculations, and web results.

name: Siri
description: Activates Siri by holding the Spotlight shortcut, or can be mapped to Cmd+Option+Space.

name: Application Switcher
description: Cycles forward through open applications in the app switcher overlay.

name: Application Switcher (Reverse)
description: Cycles backward through open applications in the app switcher overlay.

name: Force Quit Application
description: Opens the Force Quit Applications dialog to forcefully terminate unresponsive apps.

name: Quit Application
description: Quits the currently active application entirely (not just closes the window).

name: Hide Application
description: Hides the currently active application, sending it to the background without quitting.

name: Hide Other Applications
description: Hides all applications except the currently active one, clearing your visual workspace.

name: System Preferences / Settings
description: Opens System Settings (macOS 13+) or System Preferences. Typically launched via Spotlight or Apple menu.

name: Lock Screen
description: Immediately locks the screen and requires authentication to regain access.

name: Emoji & Symbols Picker
description: Opens the Character Viewer panel for browsing and inserting emoji and special Unicode characters.

name: Dictionary Lookup
description: Shows a popover dictionary definition for the word under the cursor in supported apps.

name: Screenshot — Full Screen
description: Captures the entire screen and saves it as a file on the Desktop.

name: Screenshot — Full Screen to Clipboard
description: Captures the entire screen and copies the image to the clipboard instead of saving a file.

name: Screenshot — Selected Area
description: Activates a crosshair cursor to drag and select a rectangular region to capture and save to Desktop.

name: Screenshot — Selected Area to Clipboard
description: Captures the selected area and copies the image to the clipboard instead of saving a file.

name: Screenshot — Window
description: After pressing Cmd+Shift+4, pressing Space switches to window capture mode to screenshot a specific window.

name: Screenshot — Screenshot Toolbar
description: Opens the screenshot toolbar with options for full screen, window, area capture, and screen recording.

name: Screen Recording — Start/Stop
description: Accessed via the screenshot toolbar (Cmd+Shift+5); select record entire screen or selected portion.

name: Screenshot — Touch Bar
description: Captures a screenshot of the Touch Bar on MacBook Pro models equipped with a Touch Bar.

name: Mission Control
description: Opens Mission Control, showing all open windows, spaces, and full-screen apps for easy navigation.

name: Application Exposé (Show All Windows)
description: Shows all windows of the currently active application in a spread-out Exposé view.

name: Show Desktop
description: Moves all windows aside to reveal the desktop. Press again to restore the windows.

name: Switch to Space Left
description: Moves to the desktop Space immediately to the left of the current one.

name: Switch to Space Right
description: Moves to the desktop Space immediately to the right of the current one.

name: Notification Center
mac: No default (swipe left from right edge on trackpad)
description: Opens the Notification Center sidebar; on trackpad, swipe left with two fingers from the right edge.

name: Minimize Window
description: Minimizes the current window to the Dock.

name: Zoom / Maximize Window
description: Toggles full-screen mode for the current window (app-specific). Cmd+= may zoom in some contexts.

name: Close Window / Tab
description: Closes the current window or tab. In apps with tabs, closes only the active tab.

name: Close All Windows
description: Closes all windows of the currently active application at once.

name: New Window
description: Opens a new window in the current application.

name: New Tab
description: Opens a new tab in the current application (browsers, Finder, Terminal, etc.).

name: Close Tab
description: Closes the currently active tab in apps that support tabs.

name: Undo
description: Reverses the last action or text edit in the current context.

name: Redo
description: Re-applies the last undone action. Some apps use Cmd+Y instead.

name: Cut
description: Removes the selected content and places it in the clipboard.

name: Copy
description: Copies the selected content to the clipboard without removing it.

name: Paste
description: Inserts the clipboard contents at the current cursor position.

name: Paste and Match Style
description: Pastes clipboard text without preserving the source formatting, matching the destination style.

name: Select All
description: Selects all content in the current context — text in a field, items in a list, or files in Finder.

name: Find
description: Opens the Find bar for searching text in documents, web pages, or file names in Finder.

name: Find and Replace
description: Opens the Find and Replace panel to search for text and substitute it with another string.

name: Print
description: Opens the Print dialog for the current document or page.

name: Save
description: Saves the current document. If unsaved, may prompt for a file name and location.

name: Save As
description: Saves the current document under a new name or location, leaving the original unchanged.

name: Open
description: Opens the file browser dialog to open a file in the current application.

name: Zoom In
description: Increases zoom level. In macOS Zoom accessibility feature, use Option+Cmd+= to zoom the screen.

name: Zoom Out
description: Decreases zoom level. In macOS Zoom accessibility feature, use Option+Cmd+- to zoom the screen out.

name: Toggle VoiceOver
description: Enables or disables VoiceOver, macOS's built-in screen reader for visually impaired users.

name: Safari — Open Location
description: Focuses the Safari address/search bar so you can type a URL or search query.

name: Finder — Go to Folder
description: Opens a dialog to type or paste any folder path and navigate to it directly in Finder.
